headline_marked: [[Tk 9.38 lakh crore]] budget unveiled for 2026-27, aims to revive economy
headline: Tk 9.38 lakh crore budget unveiled for 2026-27, aims to revive economy
summary: Bangladesh's new budget is 19% larger than the previous year's, with a focus on economic revival and building an inclusive society amidst significant financial challenges.
details: Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury presented a Tk 9.38 lakh crore budget for the 2026-27 fiscal year, marking the first budget by a BNP government in two decades. The fiscal plan is 19 percent larger than the previous year's original budget, aiming to stabilize and grow the economy.

The government aims to foster a discrimination-free and inclusive economy, ensuring fair participation across society. This budget is presented during a period of economic strain, with the National Board of Revenue falling short of its collection target and high non-performing loans in the banking sector.

The budget proposes raising Tk 6.95 lakh crore in revenue, with the NBR tasked to collect Tk 6.04 lakh crore, a significant challenge given current collection rates. The remaining Tk 2.43 lakh crore will be financed through borrowing and grants, projecting a budget deficit of 3.6 percent of GDP.

Key priorities include accelerating economic growth to 6.5 percent and reducing inflation to 7.5 percent. The budget also outlines a 3R strategy (Recovery and Stabilisation, Restoration, and Reconstruction for Acceleration) to revive the economy over one to five years.

Incentives for electric vehicles and buses are extended until June 30, 2030, to reduce pollution. Tax burdens on imported EVs are also proposed to be lowered, with significant reductions for vehicles priced up to $50,000.

To strengthen the capital market and reduce dependence on bank lending, the government plans to develop alternative financing sources. A TIN certificate will be mandatory for opening bank accounts, and businesses may receive licenses within seven days through a proposed single-window online platform.
caption:
Bangladesh unveils a Tk 9.38 lakh crore national budget for 2026-27.
Key goals include reviving the economy and curbing inflation.

Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury presented a Tk 9.38 lakh crore budget for the 2026-27 fiscal year, marking the first budget by a BNP government in two decades. The fiscal plan is 19 percent larger than the previous year's original budget, aiming to stabilize and grow the economy.

The government aims to foster a discrimination-free and inclusive economy, ensuring fair participation across society. This budget is presented during a period of economic strain, with the National Board of Revenue falling short of its collection target and high non-performing loans in the banking sector.

The budget proposes raising Tk 6.95 lakh crore in revenue, with the NBR tasked to collect Tk 6.04 lakh crore, a significant challenge given current collection rates. The remaining Tk 2.43 lakh crore will be financed through borrowing and grants, projecting a budget deficit of 3.6 percent of GDP.

Key priorities include accelerating economic growth to 6.5 percent and reducing inflation to 7.5 percent. The budget also outlines a 3R strategy (Recovery and Stabilisation, Restoration, and Reconstruction for Acceleration) to revive the economy over one to five years.

Incentives for electric vehicles and buses are extended until June 30, 2030, to reduce pollution. Tax burdens on imported EVs are also proposed to be lowered, with significant reductions for vehicles priced up to $50,000.

To strengthen the capital market and reduce dependence on bank lending, the government plans to develop alternative financing sources. A TIN certificate will be mandatory for opening bank accounts, and businesses may receive licenses within seven days through a proposed single-window online platform.

#BangladeshBudget #Economy #Finance #Budget2027 #AmirKhosru

Source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star
tweet: Bangladesh's Tk 9.38 lakh crore national budget for 2026-27 has been unveiled, aiming for economic revival and inflation control. The budget prioritizes inclusive growth and introduces incentives for EVs. #BangladeshBudget #Economy
source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star

headline_marked: [[Gas leak explosion]] injures five, including children, in Narayanganj home
headline: Gas leak explosion injures five, including children, in Narayanganj home
summary: A gas leak from a cylinder caused a blast in a Narayanganj residence, severely burning four members of the same family and another individual. All victims have been hospitalized.
details: An explosion rocked a home in Chandpur area of Madanpur, Bandar, Narayanganj on Thursday morning due to a gas leak. Five people, including children, sustained burn injuries in the incident.

The injured have been admitted to the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute. Among them are four members of the same family: Mannan (50), Sultana (35), Siam (19), and Mim (13). Hazrat Ali (8) is the fifth victim.

According to the Officer-in-Charge of Bandar Police Station, Golam Mostofa Ashraf Uddin, gas had accumulated inside the house from a cylinder leak. The explosion occurred when the stove was lit in the morning. Local residents rescued the injured and took them to the hospital.

Dr. Shawon Bin Rahman, resident doctor at the emergency department of the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute, reported that Sultana suffered burns on approximately 90% of her body, Siam on 77%, Mim on 41%, Mannan on 30%, and Hazrat Ali on 8%. Four patients have been admitted, while Hazrat Ali is under observation. The condition of several victims is critical.
caption:
Five people, including children, have suffered severe burns after a gas leak caused an explosion in a Narayanganj home. Some victims have critical injuries.

An explosion rocked a home in Chandpur area of Madanpur, Bandar, Narayanganj on Thursday morning due to a gas leak. Five people, including children, sustained burn injuries in the incident.

The injured have been admitted to the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute. Among them are four members of the same family: Mannan (50), Sultana (35), Siam (19), and Mim (13). Hazrat Ali (8) is the fifth victim.

According to the Officer-in-Charge of Bandar Police Station, Golam Mostofa Ashraf Uddin, gas had accumulated inside the house from a cylinder leak. The explosion occurred when the stove was lit in the morning. Local residents rescued the injured and took them to the hospital.

Dr. Shawon Bin Rahman, resident doctor at the emergency department of the National Burn and Plastic Surgery Institute, reported that Sultana suffered burns on approximately 90% of her body, Siam on 77%, Mim on 41%, Mannan on 30%, and Hazrat Ali on 8%. Four patients have been admitted, while Hazrat Ali is under observation. The condition of several victims is critical.

#Narayanganj #GasLeak #Explosion #Bangladesh #BurnInjuries #Accident

Source: Prothom Alo, Dhaka Tribune, Jamuna TV, The Daily Star
tweet: Five people, including children, suffered severe burns in a gas leak explosion at a Narayanganj home. Four family members and another individual were hospitalized, with some in critical condition. #Narayanganj #GasLeak #Bangladesh
